D7 Print L5 Evaluate Reference

PolyJet Full-Colour Resin

a sweeping print head spits picolitre droplets of cyan, magenta, yellow, white, and clear resin — a UV lamp on the same carriage cures each layer the instant it lands

PolyJet (Stratasys, Mimaki) prints with no powder. A carriage carrying many inkjet-style heads sweeps over the build, dropping picolitre droplets of UV-curable resin in CMYK plus white and clear; a UV lamp trailing on the same carriage cures each pass on the fly. Mixing droplets per voxel reproduces millions of colours without any later inking step.

Slide the build progress and watch the part stack itself, layer by layer. The process panel shows the printhead row mid-sweep, droplets falling, and the violet UV trail curing immediately behind. No powder bed; nothing to dust off afterwards.
